description:
Offered: every semester
This second-semester general college physics course consists of studies and applications of wave motions, sound, electricity, magnetism, light and optics. Typical topics include Coulomb’s law, electric force and field, potential and capacitance, electric circuits, magnetic force and field, magnetic forces on moving charges, electric induction, laws of reflection and refraction, mirrors and lenses, optical instruments, interference and diffraction of light. Cross-listed with PHYS 2700
Prerequisite(s): MATH 1300 (or equivalent) and PHYS 2300
Note: Credit cannot be earned for both PHYS 2400 and PHYS 2700.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, PL
